titles.en: The Thirtieth Assembly, Saturday, the Ninth of Muharram, 368 AH, which is the [the Eve of] the Killing of al-Husayn b. `Ali b. Abi Talib (as).

translations.bn: 1 - আবু জা'ফার মুহাম্মাদ বিন আলী বিন আল-হুসাইন বিন মূসা বিন বাবাওয়াইহ আল-কুম্মী (রাযিআল্লাহু আনহু) আমাদের হাদীস বর্ণনা করেছেন। তিনি বলেছেন: মুহাম্মাদ বিন উমার আল-বাগদাদী আল-হাফিয (রহমাতুল্লাহি আলাইহি) তাঁর বই থেকে আমাদের হাদীস বর্ণনা করেছেন। তিনি বলেছেন: ইব্রাহীম বিন উবাইদুল্লাহ আল-সাবী'ঈ, বালখের বিচারক, আমাদের হাদীস বর্ণনা করেছেন। তিনি বলেছেন: মারীসা বিনতু মূসা বিন ইউনুস — যিনি আমার ফুফু ছিলেন — আমার কাছে হাদীস বর্ণনা করেছেন। তিনি বলেছেন: সাফিয়্যা বিনতু ইউনুস আল-হামদানিয়্যা — তিনিও আমার ফুফু ছিলেন — আমার কাছে হাদীস বর্ণনা করেছেন। তিনি বলেছেন: বাহজা বিনতুল হারিস আত-তাগলিবী তাঁর মামা আব্দুল্লাহ বিন মানসুর থেকে আমার কাছে বর্ণনা করেছেন — যিনি যায়েদ বিন আলীর (আ.) কোনো পুত্রের দুধভাই ছিলেন। তিনি বলেছেন: আমি জা'ফার বিন মুহাম্মাদ বিন আলী বিন আল-হুসাইন (আলাইহিমুস সালাম)-কে জিজ্ঞেস করলাম এবং বললাম: আমাকে বলুন
translations.de: 1 - Abu Ja'far Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn b. Musa b. Babuwayh al-Qummi (möge Allah mit ihm zufrieden sein) überlieferte uns. Er sagte: Muhammad b. 'Umar al-Baghdadi al-Hafith (möge Allah ihm gnädig sein) überlieferte uns aus seinem Buch. Er sagte: Ibrahim b. 'Ubaydullah b. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Sabi'i, Richter von Balkh, überlieferte uns. Er sagte: Marsiya bt. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq — sie war meine Tante — überlieferte mir. Sie sagte: Safiyya bt. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Hamadaniyya — auch sie war meine Tante — überlieferte mir. Sie sagte: Bahja bt. al-Harith b. 'Abdullah al-Taghlubi überlieferte mir von ihrem Onkel mütterlicherseits 'Abdullah b. Mansur — der ein Milchbruder einiger Söhne von Zayd b. 'Ali (Friede sei mit ihm) war. Er sagte: Ich fragte Ja'far b. Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn (Friede sei mit ihnen) und sagte: Erzähl mir
translations.en: 1 - Abu Ja'far Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn b. Musa b. Babuwayh al-Qummi (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated to us. He said: Muhammad b. 'Umar al-Baghdadi al-Hafith (may Allah have mercy upon him) narrated to us. He said: Abu Sa'id al-Hasan b. 'Uthman b. Ziyad al-Tustari narrated to us from his book. He said: Ibrahim b. 'Ubaydullah b. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Sabi'i, the judge of Balkh, narrated to us. He said: Marsiya bt. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq narrated to me — and she was my aunt. She said: Safiyya bt. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Hamadaniyya narrated to me — and she was my aunt. She said: Bahja bt. al-Harith b. 'Abdullah al-Taghlubi narrated to me from her maternal uncle 'Abdullah b. Mansur — who was a foster-brother of some of the sons of Zayd b. 'Ali (peace be upon him). He said: I asked Ja'far b. Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn (peace be upon them) and I said: Tell me about
translations.es: 1 - Abu Ja'far Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn b. Musa b. Babuwayh al-Qummi (que Allah esté complacido con él) nos narró. Dijo: Muhammad b. 'Umar al-Baghdadi al-Hafith (que Allah tenga misericordia de él) nos narró de su libro. Dijo: Ibrahim b. 'Ubaydullah b. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Sabi'i, juez de Balkh, nos narró. Dijo: Marsiya bt. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq — que era mi tía — me narró. Dijo: Safiyya bt. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Hamadaniyya — también mi tía — me narró. Dijo: Bahja bt. al-Harith b. 'Abdullah al-Taghlubi me narró de su tío materno 'Abdullah b. Mansur — quien era hermano de leche de algunos de los hijos de Zayd b. 'Ali (la paz sea con él). Dijo: Pregunté a Ja'far b. Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn (la paz sea con ellos) y dije: Cuéntame sobre
translations.fa: 1 - ابوجعفر محمد بن علی بن الحسین بن موسی بن بابویه قمی (رضی الله عنه) برای ما روایت کرد. گفت: محمد بن عمر بغدادی الحافظ (رحمه الله) از کتاب خود برای ما روایت کرد. گفت: ابراهیم بن عبیدالله بن موسی بن یونس بن ابی اسحاق سبیعی قاضی بلخ برای ما روایت کرد. گفت: مریسه دختر موسی بن یونس بن ابی اسحاق — که عمه‌ام بود — برای من روایت کرد. گفت: صفیه دختر یونس بن ابی اسحاق همدانیه — که عمه‌ام بود — برای من روایت کرد. گفت: بهجه دختر الحارث التغلبی از دایی‌اش عبدالله بن منصور — که برادر رضاعی برخی فرزندان زید بن علی (ع) بود — برای من روایت کرد. گفت: از جعفر بن محمد بن علی بن الحسین (علیهم السلام) پرسیدم و گفتم: برایم روایت کن
translations.fr: 1 - Abu Ja'far Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn b. Musa b. Babuwayh al-Qummi (qu'Allah soit satisfait de lui) nous a rapporté. Il dit : Muhammad b. 'Umar al-Baghdadi al-Hafith (qu'Allah lui fasse miséricorde) nous a rapporté de son livre. Il dit : Ibrahim b. 'Ubaydullah b. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Sabi'i, juge de Balkh, nous a rapporté. Il dit : Marsiya bt. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq — qui était ma tante — m'a rapporté. Elle dit : Safiyya bt. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Hamadaniyya — elle aussi était ma tante — m'a rapporté. Elle dit : Bahja bt. al-Harith b. 'Abdullah al-Taghlubi m'a rapporté de son oncle maternel 'Abdullah b. Mansur — qui était le frère de lait de certains fils de Zayd b. 'Ali (paix sur lui). Il dit : J'ai interrogé Ja'far b. Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn (paix sur eux) et j'ai dit : Raconte-moi
translations.id: 1 - Abu Ja'far Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn b. Musa b. Babuwayh al-Qummi (semoga Allah meridhainya) menceritakan kepada kami. Ia berkata: Muhammad b. 'Umar al-Baghdadi al-Hafizh (semoga Allah merahmatinya) menceritakan kepada kami dari kitabnya. Ia berkata: Ibrahim b. 'Ubaydullah b. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Sabi'i, hakim Balkh, menceritakan kepada kami. Ia berkata: Marsiya bt. Musa b. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq — ia adalah bibiku — menceritakan kepadaku. Ia berkata: Shafiyya bt. Yunus b. Abi Ishaq al-Hamadaniyya — ia juga bibiku — menceritakan kepadaku. Ia berkata: Bahja bt. al-Harith b. 'Abdullah al-Taghlubi menceritakan kepadaku dari pamannya 'Abdullah b. Mansur — yang merupakan saudara sepersusuan dari sebagian putra-putra Zayd b. 'Ali (as). Ia berkata: Aku bertanya kepada Ja'far b. Muhammad b. 'Ali b. al-Husayn (as) dan berkata: Ceritakanlah kepadaku tentang
translations.ru: 1 - Абу Джа'фар Мухаммад б. 'Али б. аль-Хусейн б. Муса б. Бабавайх аль-Кумми (да будет доволен им Аллах) передал нам. Он сказал: Мухаммад б. 'Умар аль-Багдади аль-Хафиз (да помилует его Аллах) передал нам из своей книги. Он сказал: Ибрахим б. 'Убайдуллах б. Муса б. Юнус б. Аби Исхак ас-Саби'и, судья Балха, передал нам. Он сказал: Марриса бт. Муса б. Юнус б. Аби Исхак — она была моей тётей — передала мне. Она сказала: Сафийя бт. Юнус б. Аби Исхак аль-Хамаданийя — она тоже была моей тётей — передала мне. Она сказала: Бахджа бт. аль-Харис б. 'Абдуллах ат-Таглиби передала мне от своего дяди по матери 'Абдуллаха б. Мансура — который был молочным братом некоторых сыновей Зайда б. 'Али (мир ему). Он сказал: Я спросил Джа'фара б. Мухаммада б. 'Али б. аль-Хусейна (мир им) и сказал: Расскажи мне
translations.tr: 1 - Ebu Cafer Muhammed b. Ali b. el-Hüseyin b. Musa b. Babeveyh el-Kummî (Allah ondan razı olsun) bize aktardı. Dedi ki: Muhammed b. Ömer el-Bağdâdî el-Hâfız (Allah ona rahmet etsin) bize kitabından aktardı. Dedi ki: İbrahim b. Ubeydullah b. Musa b. Yunus b. Ebî İshak es-Sebi'î Belh kadısı bize aktardı. Dedi ki: Musa b. Yunus b. Ebî İshak'ın kızı Mersiye — ki o benim halamıydı — bana aktardı. Dedi ki: Yunus b. Ebî İshak el-Hemedâniyye'nin kızı Safiyye — o da benim halamıydı — bana aktardı. Dedi ki: el-Hâris b. Abdullah et-Tağlibî'nin kızı Behce, dayısı Abdullah b. Mansur'dan bana aktardı — ki o Zeyd b. Ali'nin (a.s.) oğullarından birinin süt kardeşiydi. Dedi ki: Cafer b. Muhammed b. Ali b. el-Hüseyin'e (as) sordum ve dedim: Bana anlat
translations.ur: 1 - ابوجعفر محمد بن علی بن الحسین بن موسیٰ بن بابویہ القمی (رضی اللہ عنہ) نے ہمیں حدیث بیان کی۔ انہوں نے کہا: محمد بن عمر البغدادی الحافظ (رحمہ اللہ) نے اپنی کتاب سے ہمیں حدیث بیان کی۔ انہوں نے کہا: ابراہیم بن عبیداللہ بن موسیٰ بن یونس بن ابی اسحاق السبیعی قاضی بلخ نے ہمیں حدیث بیان کی۔ انہوں نے کہا: مریسہ بنت موسیٰ بن یونس بن ابی اسحاق نے مجھے حدیث بیان کی — وہ میری پھوپھی تھیں۔ انہوں نے کہا: صفیہ بنت یونس بن ابی اسحاق الہمدانیہ نے مجھے حدیث بیان کی — وہ بھی میری پھوپھی تھیں۔ انہوں نے کہا: بہجہ بنت الحارث بن عبداللہ التغلبی نے اپنے ماموں عبداللہ بن منصور سے مجھے حدیث بیان کی — وہ زید بن علی علیہ السلام کے بعض فرزندوں کے رضاعی بھائی تھے۔ انہوں نے کہا: میں نے جعفر بن محمد بن علی بن الحسین علیہم السلام سے پوچھا اور کہا: مجھے بتائیں
translations.zh: 1 - 艾布·贾法尔·穆罕默德·本·阿里·本·侯赛因·本·穆萨·本·巴巴韦赫·古米（愿真主喜悦他）向我们传述。他说：穆罕默德·本·欧麦尔·巴格达迪·哈菲兹（愿真主怜悯他）从他的书中向我们传述。他说：巴勒赫法官易卜拉欣·本·欧贝杜拉·塞比伊向我们传述。他说：穆萨·本·尤努斯·本·艾比·伊斯哈克的女儿玛里赛——她是我的姑母——向我传述。她说：尤努斯·本·艾比·伊斯哈克的女儿萨菲娅——她也是我的姑母——向我传述。她说：哈里斯·本·阿卜杜拉·塔格里比的女儿白合嘉从她的舅舅阿卜杜拉·本·曼苏尔处向我传述——他是宰德·本·阿里（愿主赐他平安）某些儿子的奶兄弟。他说：我问了贾法尔·本·穆罕默德·本·阿里·本·侯赛因（愿主赐他们平安）并说：请告诉我

translations.en: the killing of the son of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family). So he said: My father narrated to me from his father. He said: When death came upon Mu'awiya, he called his son Yazid (may Allah curse him) and sat him before him, and said to him: 'O my son! I have subdued the difficult necks for you, consolidated the lands for you, and made the kingdom and all it contains your provision. I fear for you from three persons who will oppose you with full effort: 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. al-Khattab, 'Abdullah b. al-Zubayr, and al-Husayn b. 'Ali. As for 'Abdullah b. 'Umar, he is with you, so hold fast to him and do not leave him. As for 'Abdullah b. al-Zubayr, cut him to pieces if you overcome him, for he crouches before you as a lion crouches before its prey, and deceives you as a fox deceives a dog. As for al-Husayn, you know his standing with the Messenger of Allah — he is from the flesh and blood of the Messenger of Allah. I know with certainty that the people of Iraq will summon him to themselves, then betray him and abandon him. If you overcome him, acknowledge his right and his station with the Messenger of Allah. Do not punish him for his actions. We also have ties of blood and kinship with him. Beware of harming him or showing him anything displeasing.'

translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 3] When 'Utba heard this, he summoned a scribe and wrote: 'In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Me...
translations.en: When 'Utba heard this, he summoned a scribe and wrote: 'In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. To the servant of Allah, Yazid, Commander of the Faithful; from 'Utba b. Abi Sufyan. As for what follows: al-Husayn b. 'Ali does not recognize your caliphate or your right to a pledge of allegiance. Your view regarding his affair. Peace.' When the letter reached Yazid (may Allah curse him), he wrote the reply to 'Utba: 'As for what follows: When this letter of mine reaches you, hasten to write back to me and clarify in your letter all who are in my obedience and all who have defected. And with the reply, send the head of al-Husayn b. 'Ali.' When al-Husayn (peace be upon him) learned of this, he resolved to leave the land of the Hijaz for Iraq. When night fell, he went to the Mosque of the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) to bid farewell to the grave. When he reached the grave, a light blazed forth from it, and he returned to his place. On the second night, he went to bid farewell to the grave. He stood in prayer and prolonged it. He fell drowsy in prostration. The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) came to him in his sleep, and took al-Husayn (peace be upon him) and pressed him to his chest. He began kissing between his eyes and saying: 'May my father be your ransom! It is as though I see you covered in your blood, between a group from this nation who hope for my intercession — yet Allah has no portion for them. O my son! You are heading toward your father, your mother, and your brother, and they long for you. You have ranks in Paradise that cannot be attained except through martyrdom.'

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 4] So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) awoke from his sleep weeping. He came to his household, informed th...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 4] So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) awoke from his sleep weeping. He came to his household, informed th...
translations.en: So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) awoke from his sleep weeping. He came to his household, informed them of the dream, and bade them farewell. He loaded his sisters, his daughters, and his nephew al-Qasim b. al-Hasan b. 'Ali (peace be upon them) onto the howdahs, then set out with twenty-one men from his companions and his Ahl al-Bayt, among them: Abu Bakr b. 'Ali, Muhammad b. 'Ali, 'Uthman b. 'Ali, al-'Abbas b. 'Ali, 'Abdullah b. Muslim b. 'Aqil, 'Ali b. al-Husayn al-Akbar, and 'Ali b. al-Husayn al-Asghar. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar heard of his departure, mounted his mount, and set out hurrying behind him. He caught up with him at one of the stops and said: 'Where are you going, O son of the Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'Iraq.' He said: 'Slow down. Return to the sanctuary of your grandfather.' Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) refused. When Ibn 'Umar saw his refusal, he said: 'O Abu 'Abdillah! Show me the place where the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) used to kiss you.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) uncovered his navel, and Ibn 'Umar kissed it three times and wept, saying: 'I entrust you to Allah, O Abu 'Abdillah, for you will be killed on this journey of yours.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) and his companions set out. When they stopped at al-Tha'labiyya, a man named Bishr b. Ghalib came to him and said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! Inform me about the word of Allah: "That Day We shall summon every people with their Imam" [17:71].' He said: 'An Imam who calls to guidance, and they answer him — and an Imam who calls to misguidance, and they answer him. These are in Paradise, and those are in the Fire. That is His saying: "A party in Paradise and a party in the Blaze" [42:7].' Then he set out until he stopped at al-'Athib. He rested there at midday, then awoke from his rest weeping.

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 5] His son said to him: 'What makes you weep, my father?' He said: 'O my son! It is a moment in which d...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 5] His son said to him: 'What makes you weep, my father?' He said: 'O my son! It is a moment in which d...
translations.en: His son said to him: 'What makes you weep, my father?' He said: 'O my son! It is a moment in which dreams do not lie, and there appeared to me in my dream a voice saying: You are hastening the journey, and the journey is hastening you to Paradise.' Then he set out until he stopped at al-Rahima. A man from Kufa, called Abu Haram, came to him and said: 'O son of the Prophet! What caused you to leave Medina?' He said: 'Woe to you, O Abu Haram! They insulted my honor and I was patient. They sought my wealth and I was patient. They sought my blood and I fled. And by God, they will kill me. Then Allah will clothe them in all-encompassing humiliation and a cutting sword, and will set over them one who humiliates them.' He said: The news reached 'Ubaydullah b. Ziyad (may Allah curse him), that al-Husayn (peace be upon him) had stopped at al-Rahima. He dispatched al-Hurr b. Yazid with a thousand horsemen toward him. Al-Hurr said: 'When I left my home heading toward al-Husayn (peace be upon him), I was called three times: "O Hurr! Glad tidings of Paradise!" I looked around and saw no one. I said: May Hurr's mother be bereaved! He goes out to fight the son of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family), yet is given glad tidings of Paradise!' He caught up with them at the time of the Thuhr prayer. Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) ordered his son to call the adhan and iqama. Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) stood and led both groups in prayer together. When he finished the prayer, al-Hurr b. Yazid sprang to his feet and said: 'Peace be upon you, O son of the Messenger of Allah, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.' Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) said: 'And upon you peace. Who are you, O servant of Allah?' He said: 'I am al-Hurr b. Yazid.'

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 6] He said: 'O Hurr! Are you against us or with us?' Al-Hurr said: 'By Allah, O son of the Messenger of...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 6] He said: 'O Hurr! Are you against us or with us?' Al-Hurr said: 'By Allah, O son of the Messenger of...
translations.en: He said: 'O Hurr! Are you against us or with us?' Al-Hurr said: 'By Allah, O son of the Messenger of Allah! I was sent to fight you. I seek refuge in Allah from being resurrected from my grave with my forelock chained, my hands shackled to my neck, and my face thrown into the Fire. O son of the Messenger of Allah! Where are you going? Return to the sanctuary of your grandfather, for you will be killed.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) said [in verse]: 'I shall go forth — for there is no shame in death for a young man, if his intent is true and he strives as a Muslim. Who relieves the righteous men through his own soul, departs from the wretched and opposes the criminal. If I die, I will not regret; if I live, I will not be blamed. It is shame enough for you to die in humiliation and submission.' Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) set out until he stopped at al-Qatqatana. He saw a pavilion pitched there and said: 'To whom does this pavilion belong?' It was said: 'To 'Ubaydullah b. al-Hurr al-Ju'fi.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) sent for him and said: 'O man! You are a sinner in error, and Allah will surely take you to account for what you do if you do not repent to Allah this very moment, and support me — that my grandfather may intercede for you before Allah.' He said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! By Allah, if I supported you, I would be the first to be killed before you. But here is my horse — take it.' Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) turned his face away from him and said: 'We have no need of you or your horse. I would not take the misguided as supporters. Leave, and do not be with us nor against us. Surely, whoever hears our cry — we the Ahl al-Bayt — and does not respond, Allah will throw him face-first into Hell.' Then he set out until he stopped in Karbala'. He said: 'What place is this?' It was said: 'This is Karbala', O son of the Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'By Allah, this is a day of distress and affliction (karb wa bala'). This is the place where our blood will be shed and our sacred inviolability violated.' Then 'Ubaydullah b. Ziyad came with his army and encamped at al-Nakhila. He sent 'Umar b. Sa'd to al-Husayn commanding four thousand horsemen.

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 7] 'Abdullah b. al-Husayn al-Tamimi came with a thousand horsemen, followed by Shabath b. Rab'i with a ...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 7] 'Abdullah b. al-Husayn al-Tamimi came with a thousand horsemen, followed by Shabath b. Rab'i with a ...
translations.en: 'Abdullah b. al-Husayn al-Tamimi came with a thousand horsemen, followed by Shabath b. Rab'i with a thousand horsemen, and Muhammad b. al-Ash'ath b. Qays al-Kindi also with a thousand horsemen. 'Umar b. Sa'd was given authority over the people and they were commanded to hear and obey him. 'Ubaydullah b. Ziyad was informed that 'Umar b. Sa'd was fraternizing with al-Husayn and was reluctant to fight him. So he dispatched Shimr b. Dhil Jawshan with four thousand horsemen and wrote: 'When this letter reaches you, do not delay with al-Husayn b. 'Ali. Seize him and cut off the water from him.' When the letter reached 'Umar b. Sa'd (may Allah curse him), he ordered his herald to announce: 'We have given al-Husayn and his companions their day and their night.' Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) stood and addressed his companions: 'O Allah! Surely I know of no household more righteous, more pure, and more clean than my household; nor companions better than my companions. You see what has befallen us. You are released from your pledge of allegiance to me. This night has enveloped you, so take it as your mount and scatter in its darkness, for indeed the people only want me.' 'Abdullah b. Muslim b. 'Aqil b. Abi Talib stood and said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! What would the people say of us if we abandoned our elder and our master without fighting alongside him?! No, by Allah! We will give our lives for your life and our blood for your blood.' A man named Zuhayr b. al-Qayn al-Bajali stood and said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! I wish I were killed and revived for you a hundred times so that Allah might defend you, the Ahl al-Bayt, through me.' Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) ordered a trench to be dug around his camp and filled with firewood. Then he sent his son 'Ali (peace be upon him) with thirty horsemen and twenty foot soldiers to fetch water. Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) began to recite [verse]: 'O Time, shame on you as a companion! How many a seeker and companion you have slain! The matter belongs to the Majestic alone. Every living soul treads my path.' Then he said to his companions: 'Stand and drink water — let it be your last provision. Perform ablution, bathe, and wash your garments, for they will be your shrouds.'

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 8] Then he led them in Fajr prayer and deployed them for battle. He called for the trench to be set ali...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 8] Then he led them in Fajr prayer and deployed them for battle. He called for the trench to be set ali...
translations.en: Then he led them in Fajr prayer and deployed them for battle. He called for the trench to be set alight so the enemy could only attack from one direction. A horseman from the army of 'Umar b. Sa'd came forward, named Ibn Abi Juwayriyya al-Mazani. When he saw the fire blazing, he clapped his hands and called out: 'O Husayn! I give you tidings of the Fire — you have hastened it in this world!' Al-Husayn said: 'O Allah! Let him taste the punishment of the Fire in this world.' So his horse threw him into that fire and he burned. Then another man named Tamim b. Husayn al-Fazari called out: 'O Husayn! Do you not see the Euphrates? By Allah, you will not taste a drop of it until you drink death in gulps!' Al-Husayn said: 'O Allah! Kill him of thirst on this day.' So thirst choked him until he fell from his horse and was trampled to death. Then Muhammad b. al-Ash'ath b. Qays al-Kindi said: 'O Husayn son of Fatima! What sanctity do you have from the Messenger of Allah that others do not?' So al-Husayn recited: 'Truly, Allah chose Adam, Noah, the family of Ibrahim, and the family of 'Imran above all worlds — descendants of one another' [3:33-34]. Then he said: 'By Allah, surely Muhammad is from the family of Ibrahim, and the guided progeny is from the family of Muhammad.' Al-Husayn raised his head to the sky and said: 'O Allah! Show Muhammad b. al-Ash'ath humiliation this day.' Then the man left to relieve himself, and Allah sent a scorpion to sting him, and he died. Thirst then overtook al-Husayn and his companions. A man named Burayr b. Khudayr al-Hamadani came to him and said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! Do you permit me to go speak to them?' He permitted him, so he said: 'O people! Surely Allah sent Muhammad in truth. This water of the Euphrates has been placed between him and the son of his Prophet.' They said: 'O Burayr! By Allah, al-Husayn will die of thirst just as others did.' Al-Husayn said: 'Sit, O Burayr.'

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 9] Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) rose, leaning on his sword, and called out at the top of his voic...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 9] Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) rose, leaning on his sword, and called out at the top of his voic...
translations.en: Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) rose, leaning on his sword, and called out at the top of his voice: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know me?' They said: 'Yes. You are the son of the Messenger of Allah and his grandson.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that my grandfather is the Messenger of Allah?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that my mother is Fatima the daughter of Muhammad?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that my father is 'Ali b. Abi Talib?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that my grandmother Khadija bt. Khuwaylid was the first woman of this nation to embrace Islam?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that the Master of Martyrs, Hamza, is the uncle of my father?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that Ja'far who flies in Paradise is my uncle?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that this is the sword of the Messenger of Allah that I wear?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that this turban I am wearing is the turban of the Messenger of Allah?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'I adjure you by Allah — do you know that 'Ali was the first to embrace Islam, the most knowledgeable, the greatest in forbearance, and the Master of every believing man and woman?' They said: 'O Allah, yes.' He said: 'Then by what do you make my blood permissible — when my father will be at the Pond, while the Banner of Praise is in the hand of my grandfather on the Day of Resurrection?' They said: 'We know all of this, and we will not leave you until you die of thirst.' Al-Husayn grasped his beard — he was fifty-seven years old that day — and said: 'Allah's wrath intensified against the Jews who said 'Uzayr is the son of Allah. Allah's wrath intensified against the Christians who said the Messiah is the son of Allah. Allah's wrath intensified against the Zoroastrians who worshiped fire. Allah's wrath intensified against those who killed their prophet. Allah's wrath will intensify against this group who want to kill the son of their prophet.'

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 10] Then al-Hurr b. Yazid spurred his horse and crossed to the camp of al-Husayn (peace be upon him), wi...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 10] Then al-Hurr b. Yazid spurred his horse and crossed to the camp of al-Husayn (peace be upon him), wi...
translations.en: Then al-Hurr b. Yazid spurred his horse and crossed to the camp of al-Husayn (peace be upon him), with his hand upon his head saying: 'O Allah! I repent to You — forgive me, for I have terrified the hearts of Your friends and the children of Your Prophet. O son of the Messenger of Allah! Is there repentance for me?' He said: 'Yes. Allah has accepted your repentance.' He said: 'Do you permit me to fight on your behalf?' He granted him permission. He went out saying: 'I will strike your necks with the sword, for the best one who dwelt in the land of al-Khayf.' He killed eighteen men, then was killed. Al-Husayn came to him and said: 'Bravo, O Hurr! You are truly free, just as you were named, in this world and the next.' Then al-Husayn recited [verse in praise of al-Hurr]. Then Zuhayr b. al-Qayn al-Bajali set out, saying: 'Today we shall meet your grandfather the Prophet, and Hasan, and 'Ali.' He killed nineteen, then was struck down. Then Habib b. Mathahir al-Asadi set out, saying: 'We are more pristine than you — we support the best of people.' He killed thirty-one, then was killed. Then 'Abdullah b. Abi 'Urwa al-Ghifari set out and killed twenty, then was killed. Then Burayr b. Khudayr — the finest Quran reciter of his time — set out and killed thirty, then was killed. Then Malik b. Anas al-Kahili set out, saying: 'The clan of 'Ali are the Shi'a of the Beneficent, and the clan of Harb are the Shi'a of Satan.' He killed eighteen, then was killed. Then Ziyad b. Mahasir al-Kindi set out and killed nine, then was killed. Then Wahb b. Wahb — a Christian who had converted at the hands of al-Husayn along with his mother — fought and killed seven or eight, then was captured and beheaded by order of 'Umar b. Sa'd. His head was thrown toward al-Husayn's camp. His mother took his sword. Al-Husayn said to her: 'O Umm Wahb! Sit, for Allah has lifted jihad from women. You and your son are with my grandfather in Paradise.' Then Hilal b. Hajjaj set out and killed thirteen, then was killed. Then 'Abdullah b. Muslim b. 'Aqil set out and killed three, then was killed. Then 'Ali b. al-Husayn al-Asghar set out. Al-Husayn wept and said: 'O Allah! Be the witness.' He killed ten, then returned saying: 'O father! Thirst!' Al-Husayn said: 'Be patient, my son — your grandfather will give you to drink from the most fulfilling cup.' He returned and killed forty-four, then was killed. Then al-Qasim b. al-Hasan b. 'Ali set out, saying: 'Do not grieve — today you will attain Paradise.' He killed three, then was thrown from his horse.

translations.bn: [বাংলা অনুবাদ - অংশ 11] Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) looked right and left and saw no one remaining. He raised his head to ...
translations.de: [Deutsche Übersetzung - Teil 11] Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) looked right and left and saw no one remaining. He raised his head to ...
translations.en: Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) looked right and left and saw no one remaining. He raised his head to the sky and said: 'O Allah! You see what is being done to the descendants of Your Prophet.' The Banu Kilab came between him and the water. An arrow was shot and struck his throat. He fell from his horse. He took the arrow and cast it away. He tried to catch the blood with his hand until his palm was full, then smeared it on his head and beard, saying: 'I will meet Allah, Almighty and Majestic, oppressed and smeared in my own blood.' Then he fell upon his left cheek. The enemies of Allah — Sinan b. Anas al-Iyadi and Shimr b. Dhil Jawshan (may Allah curse them both) — advanced with men from the Levant. Some said to one another: 'What are you waiting for? Put the man to rest.' Sinan b. Anas came down, took al-Husayn's beard, and began striking his throat with the sword, saying: 'By Allah! I cut off your head knowing you are the son of the Messenger of Allah and that you have the best father and mother.' The horse of al-Husayn came forward with its mane covered in his blood, running and neighing. The daughters of the Prophet heard its neighs and saw the horse without a rider. They knew al-Husayn had been killed. Umm Kulthum bt. al-Husayn came out, wailing: 'O Muhammada! This is al-Husayn in the open, stripped of his turban and cloak.' Sinan came forward until he brought the head of al-Husayn b. 'Ali to 'Ubaydullah b. Ziyad, saying [verse]: 'Fill my saddlebag with silver and gold, for I have killed the veiled king; I have killed the one with the best mother and father, and the best lineage.' So 'Ubaydullah said to him: 'Woe to you! If you knew he has the best father and mother, why did you kill him?!' He ordered his neck to be struck, and Allah hastened his soul to the Fire. Then Ibn Ziyad sent for Umm Kulthum bt. al-Husayn and said: 'Praise be to Allah who killed your men — so how do you see what has happened to you?' She said: 'O Ibn Ziyad! If your eye is gladdened by the killing of al-Husayn, then the eye of his grandfather has long been gladdened by him — he used to kiss him and carry him on his shoulders. O Ibn Ziyad! Prepare an answer for his grandfather, for he will be your adversary tomorrow.'

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُوسَى بْنِ بَابَوَيْهِ الْقُمِّيُّ (رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ)، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُمَرَ الْبَغْدَادِيُّ الْحَافِظُ (رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ)، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ زِيَادٍ التُّسْتَرِيُّ مِنْ كِتَابِهِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُوسَى بْنِ يُونُسَ بْنِ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ السَّبِيعِيِّ قَاضِي بَلْخٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَتْنِي مَرِيسَةُ بِنْتُ مُوسَى بْنِ يُونُسَ بْنِ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ وَكَانَتْ عَمَّتِي، قَالَتْ: حَدَّثَتْنِي صَفِيَّةُ بِنْتُ يُونُسَ بْنِ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ الْهَمْدَانِيَّةُ وَكَانَتْ عَمَّتِي، قَالَتْ: حَدَّثَتْنِي بَهْجَةُ بِنْتُ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ التَّغْلِبِيُّ، عَنْ خَالِهَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَنْصُورٍ وَكَانَ رَضِيعًا لِبَعْضِ وَلَدِ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ (عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ)، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ جَعْفَرَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ (عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ)

summaries.bn: এই হাদীসটি ইমাম জা'ফার সাদিক (আ.) তাঁর পিতা ও পিতামহের সূত্রে ইমাম হুসাইন (আ.)-এর কারবালার ঘটনার সম্পূর্ণ বিবরণ দিয়েছেন। মু'আওয়িয়ার ওয়াসিয়ত থেকে শুরু হয়ে বাই'আত প্রত্যাখ্যান, নবীর কবরের স্বপ্ন, কারবালায় পানি বন্ধ, সঙ্গীদের শাহাদাত এবং উম্মে কুলসুমের ইবনে যিয়াদকে দেওয়া জবাব পর্যন্ত বিস্তৃত।
summaries.de: Dieser Hadith ist ein vollständiger Bericht über die Ereignisse von Karbala, überliefert von Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (Friede sei mit ihm) durch seinen Vater und Großvater. Er beginnt mit dem Sterbebett Mu'awiyas und seinem Rat an Yazid, folgt al-Husayns Verweigerung der Huldigung, seinem prophetischen Traum, der Ankunft in Karbala, der Wassersperre, dem Martyrium der Gefährten nacheinander, dem eigenen Martyrium al-Husayns und der mutigen Antwort von Umm Kulthum an Ibn Ziyad.
summaries.en: This hadith, narrated by Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS) through his father and grandfather, recounts the full account of the martyrdom of Imam al-Husayn b. 'Ali (AS) in Karbala (61 AH). It opens with Mu'awiya's deathbed counsel to Yazid, warning him about al-Husayn while paradoxically counseling restraint. The narrative follows al-Husayn's refusal to pledge allegiance to Yazid, his farewell visit to the Prophet's grave where he received a prophetic dream foretelling his martyrdom, his journey through the Arabian desert, his encounter with Hurr b. Yazid, the arrival at Karbala, Ibn Ziyad's siege and water-blockade, al-Husayn's famous address adjuring his enemies by their own acknowledgment of his noble lineage, the individual martyrdoms of his companions and family members one by one, his own martyrdom, and the final defiant words of Umm Kulthum to Ibn Ziyad. This is one of the most comprehensive early Shia accounts of Karbala, transmitted through an extraordinary chain including three female narrators, reflecting the event's importance to the early Muslim community around 368 AH.
summaries.es: Este hadiz es un relato completo de los eventos de Karbala narrado por el Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (la paz sea con él) a través de su padre y su abuelo. Comienza con el lecho de muerte de Mu'awiya y su consejo a Yazid, y continúa con el rechazo de la bai'a por al-Husayn, su sueño profético, la llegada a Karbala, el bloqueo del agua, el martirio de los compañeros uno a uno, el propio martirio de al-Husayn, y la respuesta valiente de Umm Kulthum a Ibn Ziyad.
summaries.fa: این حدیث روایت کاملی است از واقعه کربلا که امام جعفر صادق (ع) از پدران خود نقل کرده است. از وصیت معاویه به یزید آغاز می‌شود، به رد بیعت توسط حسین (ع)، خواب پیامبر، سفر به کربلا، قطع آب، شهادت یاران، و پاسخ کوبنده ام کلثوم به ابن زیاد می‌رسد.
summaries.fr: Ce hadith est un récit complet des événements de Karbala, narré par l'Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (paix sur lui) à travers son père et son grand-père. Il commence par le lit de mort de Mu'awiya et ses conseils à Yazid, puis suit le refus de bay'a d'al-Husayn, son rêve prophétique, l'arrivée à Karbala, le blocus de l'eau, le martyre des compagnons l'un après l'autre, le martyre d'al-Husayn lui-même, et la réponse courageuse d'Umm Kulthum à Ibn Ziyad.
summaries.id: Hadis ini adalah riwayat lengkap tentang peristiwa Karbala yang dinukilkan oleh Imam Ja'far al-Shadiq (as) dari ayah dan kakeknya. Mulai dari wasiat Mu'awiya kepada Yazid, penolakan bai'at oleh al-Husayn, mimpi beliau, perjalanan ke Karbala, pemblokiran air, gugurnya para sahabat satu per satu, syahadah al-Husayn sendiri, hingga jawaban tegas Umm Kulthum kepada Ibn Ziyad.
summaries.ru: Этот хадис является полным рассказом о событиях в Кербеле, переданным Имамом Джа'фаром ас-Садиком (мир ему) через своего отца и деда. Он начинается со смертного ложа Му'авии и его советов Язиду, следует за отказом аль-Хусейна от бай'а, его пророческим сном, прибытием в Кербелу, блокадой воды, мученичеством сподвижников одного за другим, собственным мученичеством аль-Хусейна и смелым ответом Умм Кульсум Ибн Зияду.
summaries.tr: İmam Cafer es-Sâdık (as), babası ve dedesinin vasıtasıyla İmam Hüseyin'in (as) Kerbela'daki şehadetinin tüm olaylarını aktarmıştır. Bu hadis, Muaviye'nin ölüm döşeğindeki vasiyetinden başlayarak Yezid'in biat talebine, Peygamber'in kabrini veda ziyaretine, Kerbela'ya varışa, su ablukasına, ashâbın birer birer şehit düşmesine ve son olarak Ümmü Külsüm'ün İbn Ziyad'a verdiği güçlü cevaba kadar uzanmaktadır.
summaries.ur: امام جعفر صادق علیہ السلام نے اپنے والد اور دادا کے واسطے سے شہادت امام حسین علیہ السلام کا مفصل واقعہ بیان فرمایا۔ یہ روایت معاویہ کی وصیت سے شروع ہو کر یزید کی بیعت طلبی، آپ کے خواب، سفر کربلا، پانی کی بندش، اصحاب کی شہادت، اور آخر میں ام کلثوم کے ابن زیاد کو جواب تک محیط ہے۔
summaries.zh: 这段圣训是伊玛目贾法尔·萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）通过其父亲和祖父传述的卡尔巴拉事件的完整记录。从穆阿维叶的临终遗嘱开始，经过侯赛因拒绝效忠、先知的预兆之梦、抵达卡尔巴拉、断水围困、同伴们相继殉道，直至侯赛因本人殉道，以及乌姆·库尔苏姆对伊本·齐亚德的有力回应。
